seo.title: Building Research Agents with Flowise + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Flowise + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Flowise enables rapid development of Research Agents through its drag-and-drop interface for multi-step web search, retrieval, and synthesis pipelines. These agents excel at gathering and summarizing information but are prone to issues like unvalidated outputs, irrelevant retrievals, and context loss in agent handoffs. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify source accuracy, claim validity, recency, and bias in real-time, ensuring reliable research outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Flowise
architectureOverview.description:
Flowise Research Agents typically start with a user query triggering a Web Search Tool node that fetches results from search APIs like Tavily or Google. These results feed into a Vector Store Retriever (e.g., Pinecone or Weaviate) for RAG augmentation, followed by an LLM Chain for initial analysis. A multi-agent supervisor then orchestrates synthesis agents that cross-verify claims, check recency, and detect bias before final summarization.

The drag-and-drop canvas connects these nodes sequentially: Query вҶ’ Search вҶ’ Embed & Retrieve вҶ’ Analyze вҶ’ Verify вҶ’ Summarize. Custom tool nodes handle specialized tasks like fact-checking APIs or bias scoring. Context flows through memory buffers, but handoffs between agents often lose critical intermediate state.

Vex integrates as a callback handler at key checkpoints: after retrieval, post-LLM generation, and before final output, enabling verification without disrupting the low-code workflow.

risks.name: Chatflow chains producing unvalidated intermediate outputs
risks.description: LLM nodes generate analysis without source verification, leading to hallucinated claims propagating through the pipeline
risks.mitigation: Vex source verification callbacks after every LLM generation node

risks.name: Vector store retrieval returning low-relevance documents
risks.description: RAG retrievers return outdated or off-topic documents due to embedding drift or poor chunking
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic relevance scoring and recency checks on retrieved chunks

risks.name: Custom tool nodes failing silently without error propagation
risks.description: Web search or fact-check tools timeout or return empty results without triggering flow errors
risks.mitigation: Vex tool result validation with fallback routing

risks.name: Multi-agent sequential flow losing context between agent handoffs
risks.description: Supervisor-agent handoffs drop critical verification metadata and source trails
risks.mitigation: Vex context preservation middleware in memory buffers
